what_it_does: This file is a JSON ledger of extracted layout-zone findings from a set of ConceptV pitch decks and landing-page mockups. Each entry names a spatial zone, describes its visual recipe, cites observed evidence, labels whether it is emergent, and provides a prescriptive generator rule, along with the source artifact and compression depth.
observations: Every entry has category 'zone' and follows the same schema: name, recipe, evidence, emergent flag, generator_rule, _src, _depth.
Findings span many source artifacts: capital-raise, company-info, deck1-2026, pitch-deck, presentation-15p, product-vi-landing, product-vt, recent-pitches.
Depths range from 1-page compressed one-pagers to 18-page, 15-page, 30-page, and long-form 49-page decks, plus landing pages.
Many zones recur across sources with similar intent but different measurements, e.g., title zones, footer/V-mark zones, split-layout zones.
Specific dimensional and color tokens are recorded, such as #F0F5F0 pale green, #faf8f0 cream, gold V-mark sizes, and percentage heights.
Some zones are flagged emergent=true while others are non-emergent, suggesting a distinction between invariant patterns and observed special cases.
The file mixes presentation-slide zones and web-landing-page zones in a single flat array.
Generator rules are imperative and often include content budgets, spatial constraints, and prohibitions.
standouts: Persistent footer/V-mark zone treated as sacred brand-only space across nearly all content slides.
Left-visual / right-content split and left/right before-after splits used as narrative-rhetorical devices.
Audience-calibrated zones, e.g., stat-proof box for architects versus lifestyle photography for residential.
Market-vertical template with fixed four-quadrant geometry reused across verticals.
Problem-solution dyad with dashed arrow as core one-pager persuasion structure.
Inline ConceptV wordmark used as a brand-claim subject within body text.
conventions: Recipe describes what the zone looks like; generator_rule tells how to reproduce it.
Evidence is given as page numbers or strip identifiers.
Provenance is captured in _src and _depth fields.
Colors and sizes are given as approximate ranges or hex values.
Repeated use of 'sacred', 'never', and 'always' in generator rules to enforce exclusivity.
Distinguishes full-bleed photo slides from grid-based content slides.
concerns: Several zones share the same name, e.g., 'Title zone' and 'Footer zone', with slightly different measurements and rules, which could create ambiguity for a generator.
V-mark/logo sizes and footer compositions vary across sources without explicit guidance on which to apply when.
The flat array has no IDs or explicit relationships, so zone composition within a single slide is implicit.
The 'emergent' flag is not defined; its basis is unclear from the file alone.
Some evidence references page numbers without the corresponding slide images or context.
Landing-page and deck zones are mixed without a medium discriminator field.
notes: A flat, source-annotated catalog of ConceptV spatial layout patterns that tries to turn visual observations into reproducible generator rules across many document depths and audiences.
questions: How should a generator resolve conflicting measurements for identically named zones from different sources?
Is the 'emergent' flag manually assigned or inferred by an extraction process?
What is the intended consumer of this file: a layout generator, a design reviewer, or a fusion index?
How are zones intended to be composed together within a single slide?
Are the color and size values approximate heuristics or exact specifications?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is a ledger of extracted layout-zone findings with recipes and generator rules, which is exactly what its content shows.
apparent_themes: Brand consistency through repeated sacred zones.
Compression-invariance of core zones across 1-page, 15-page, and 30-page decks.
Whitespace and footer exclusivity as brand-imprint devices.
Templates as evidence of system scalability.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to encode a reusable, source-agnostic design language for ConceptV presentations and landing pages so that a generative system can reproduce consistent spatial patterns and brand behavior across varying depths and audiences.
novelty: It treats layout zones as semantic and rhetorical devices rather than purely geometric regions, and it unifies pitch-deck and landing-page spatial patterns in one prescriptive ledger.
contribution: A structured inventory of spatial patterns with provenance, measurements, and generator rules that a fused system can use to enforce layout grammar, brand-zone exclusivity, and audience-aware composition.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger of layout-zone findings extracted from multiple ConceptV pitch decks and landing pages, each entry naming a zone, describing its visual recipe, citing evidence, and providing a prescriptive generator rule with source and compression depth.
intention_for_embedding: To capture reusable spatial layout patterns and brand-zone rules for generative reproduction across varying document depths and audiences.
